spec_match.rationale: The page now uses <usermedia> slots with setConstraints() and stream/error/cancel listeners instead of the unrelated <permission> element.

interactive.rationale: Users can start video or audio capture, inspect negotiated settings and capabilities, enumerate devices, switch video input, capture a still frame, and stop streams.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede and labels describe the UserMedia capability element and its fallback behavior rather than the old Permission Element feature.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo handles unsupported native elements, permission denial, prompt cancellation, empty device lists, and stream cleanup.

references.rationale: The ChromeStatus link now points to feature 4926233538330624.

design_system.rationale: The page keeps the showcase layout and control styling while using safe DOM text insertion for dynamic device data.

summary: The concept has been realigned from HTMLPermissionElement to <usermedia> while preserving the useful stream inspection and capture tooling.
